text:
Samoa's national boxing coach, Lionel Elika Fatupaito, tragically passed away at the age of 60 following a cardiac arrest in Paris' Olympic village on Friday.

Fatupaito was taken ill around 10.20am local time in his room, just prior to the commencement of the opening ceremony. The athlete accompanying him at the time immediately called for help; however, despite attempts by emergency services, he was sadly declared dead at the scene from "natural causes".

The unfortunate incident was confirmed by the International Boxing Association (IBA) via a statement released on Saturday. An IBA representative expressed their condolences stating: "We at the IBA extend our heartfelt condolences to the family, friends, and colleagues of Lionel Elika Fatupaito, the national boxing coach of Samoa, who tragically passed away during the Paris 2024.

"Lionel's dedication and passion for the sport have left an indelible mark on the boxing community. His legacy will continue to inspire future generations. Our thoughts and prayers are with Team Samoa and all those affected by this profound loss."

Pauga Talalelei Pauga, the chairperson of the Samoa National Olympic Committee, expressed a tribute claiming: "Lionel was one of Samoa's top boxing coaches and a great believer in the Olympic ideal.

"Our thoughts and those of the entire Samoan sporting community are with Lionel's family, his boxers, and friends. He will be greatly missed."

Fatupaito travelled to Paris to coach Samoa's only boxing contender, heavyweight Ato Plodzicki-Faoagali. The 25-year-old has two silver medals from the Commonwealth Games and a pair of golds from the Pacific Games under his belt.

Plodzicki-Faoagali is due to begin his Olympic journey in the preliminary round of the boxing tournament on Sunday, where he will face off against Belgium's Victor Schelstraete.

title: Boxing coach found dead in Olympic village as tributes pour in | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Sam Frost
description: Lionel Elika Fatupaito, who was the boxing coach for Samoa at the Paris Olympics, has died at the age of 60 after suffering a cardiac arrest in the Olympic village
